name: Material Adaptation Strategy
consequences: Choosing advanced composites reduces construction time but increases dependency on specialized suppliers, risking systemic supply chain vulnerabilities and strategic isolation from local resources.
options: Utilize locally sourced reinforced concrete for cost efficiency
Implement hybrid steel-aluminum alloys for moderate weight and durability
Adopt 3D-printed bio-composites using in-silo organic waste
review: Controls Cost vs. Resilience. Weakness: The options fail to consider geological compatibility of materials with underground stability.

name: Surveillance Optimization Framework
consequences: Deploying AI-driven monitoring enables real-time threat detection but creates systemic risks of data overload and strategic reliance on proprietary algorithms for social control.
options: Install traditional CCTV networks with human oversight
Integrate AI-powered behavioral analytics for predictive enforcement
Develop quantum-encrypted decentralized surveillance nodes
review: Controls Security vs. Privacy. Weakness: The options neglect the psychological impact of constant monitoring on resident compliance.

name: Energy Autonomy Model
consequences: Prioritizing nuclear fission ensures 25% faster scaling through stable baseload power but creates systemic political tensions with external energy providers and strategic risks of radioactive contamination.
options: Build geothermal plants using subterranean heat sources
Deploy solar-thermal arrays in upper levels with energy storage
Test experimental fusion reactors for long-term sustainability
review: Controls Reliability vs. Safety. Weakness: The options understate the technical challenges of maintaining fusion systems in confined spaces.

name: Social Control Protocol

review: Controls Order vs. Innovation. Weakness: The options ignore the potential for emergent leadership structures outside formal hierarchies.
